explanations.22: raise_for_status() throws an error if the HTTP request failed (like a 404 or 500), so we don't accidentally use bad data.
explanations.34: The ['---'] * len(headers) creates a list of dashes repeated as many times as there are column headers, which Markdown needs to separate the table header from the body rows.
explanations.37: We're diving into nested dictionaries here—user['name'] is itself a dictionary containing 'first' and 'last' keys, so we have to go two levels deep to get the actual name values.
